reasoning: The Federal Reserve is anticipated to be in an easing or neutral cycle through late 2025 and 2026. With the 4-week secondary market treasury bill rate around 4.020% in October 2025, the overarching trend is more likely downward or sideways rather than upward. Rate cuts are generally expected to outnumber hikes during this period. While day-to-day liquidity conditions can cause minor upward spikes, a sustained structural increase by January 2026 (or related resolution dates) compared to October 2025 remains unlikely. I estimate a 25% chance of an increase, reflecting the asymmetrical downside risk to short-term interest rates.
